tutorial.title: Interactive tutorial
tutorial.intro: Six short coding exercises with a live training arena.

1.title: Movement and turns
1.brief: Guide the blue tank to point B. The tank only moves forward, so turn it before changing direction.
1.hint: Move up twice, turn right, then move one more cell.
1.retry: The tank has not reached point B. Check the number of moves and the turn direction.

2.title: Radar scan
2.brief: Detect the red tank without firing. scan() sees forward in a straight line until a wall blocks the signal.
2.hint: The tank already faces the target. Call scan().
2.retry: The radar did not detect a target. Make sure scan() is executed while facing the enemy.

3.title: Combat condition
3.brief: Use a condition so the tank fires only when its radar sees an enemy.
3.hint: Replace pass inside if scan(): with fire().
3.retry: Use if scan(): and destroy the visible enemy from inside its block.

4.title: Command loops
4.brief: Reach point B without writing move() four times. Put the movement command inside a loop.
4.hint: Use for i in range(4): and indent move() by four spaces.
4.retry: The tank has not reached point B. Verify the loop count and indentation.

5.title: Break the wall
5.brief: A brick wall blocks the firing line. Destroy it with one correctly aimed shot.
5.hint: The barrel already points at the brick. Use fire().
5.retry: The brick is still intact. Fire while the barrel points to the right.

6.title: Multiple enemies
6.brief: Destroy both red tanks. Check the first direction, fire, then turn toward the second target and check again.
6.hint: After the first shot, rotate left and use a second if scan(): block.
6.retry: At least one enemy remains. Scan and fire in both directions.

home.meta: Program your tank, destroy walls, and battle AI or other players.
home.heroText: Write tank-control programs, complete missions, and test your algorithms against AI and other players.
home.chooseModeText: Three modes—from learning the basics to competing with player-written algorithms.
home.campaignText: Start with a route from point A to B. Learn to rotate, use loops, scan the field, and battle multiple enemies.
home.victory: Your code. Your tank. Your victory.
home.interfaceText: Use a small, clear command set, then combine commands with conditions and loops.
home.mechanicsText: Every mechanic is visible on the field and affects the result of your program.

01.title: MISSION MODE
01.description: Progress from basic movement to battling three autonomous enemy tanks at once.

02.title: CHALLENGE VS AI
02.description: Write a strategy in 60 seconds and test it against Easy, Medium, or Hard AI.

03.title: PLAYER VS PLAYER
03.description: Create a room, share the invitation code, and battle player-written strategies in real time.

01.title: WRITE
01.text: Build a strategy from commands, conditions, and loops.

02.title: VALIDATE
02.text: The safe interpreter checks your code and its limits.

03.title: DEPLOY
03.text: The tank executes your commands on a 10×8 grid arena.

04.title: IMPROVE
04.text: Study the battle log, fix your algorithm, and win.

0.title: GRID MOVEMENT
0.text: Each move() advances the tank exactly one cell.

1.title: BRICK WALLS
1.text: Brick breaks with one hit and opens a new route.

2.title: STEEL BLOCKS
2.text: Steel cannot be destroyed; go around it or use it as cover.

3.title: PROJECTILES
3.text: Bullets collide, disappear, and cannot pass through tanks.

4.title: HP SYSTEM
4.text: Hits reduce HP, and a damaged tank flashes red.

5.title: LIVE AI
5.text: Enemies move, seek firing lines, and destroy brick walls.

missions.title: The tank commander's path
missions.intro: Write a program that controls your tank. Reach point B in early missions; in combat missions, destroy the enemy first.

1.title: Straight route
1.objective: Move the tank from A (1,6) to B (1,2).
1.hint: Use move() and a range() loop.

2.title: First turn
2.objective: Move the tank from A (1,6) to B (5,2).
2.hint: Move upward first, then turn right.

3.title: Commander's route
3.objective: Move the tank from A (1,6) to B (8,1).
3.hint: Build the route with two loops and one turn.

4.title: First battle
4.objective: Destroy the red tank and capture point B (7,1).
4.hint: Use scan(), rotation, and accurate shots.

5.title: Fire corridor
5.objective: Destroy the brick cover, eliminate the enemy, and reach B (8,2).
5.hint: Steel cannot be destroyed—find a firing line through brick.

6.title: Steel fortress
6.objective: Defeat the reinforced 150 HP tank and capture B (8,1).
6.hint: Several hits are required. Use walls as cover.

7.title: Double threat
7.objective: Destroy two enemy tanks and reach B (8,3).
7.hint: Watch both attack directions and use cover.

8.title: Crossfire
8.objective: Defeat two tanks attacking from different sides and capture B (8,3).

9.title: Triple siege
9.objective: Destroy three enemy tanks and reach point B (8,3).
9.hint: Destroy the closest enemy first, then change position and firing line.

maps.pvpAuthRequired: Sign in with Google or GitHub to create or join a protected PvP room.
maps.challengeIntro: Each map uses the same AI, but the block layout changes routes and lines of fire.
maps.pvpIntro: The map is saved with the room. Both players see the same walls and battle in the selected arena.

1.title: Classic arena
1.challengeDescription: Symmetrical fortifications and a central square.
1.pvpDescription: Symmetrical fortifications and a central square for a fair opening battle.

2.title: Twin corridors
2.challengeDescription: Long passages and positions for ranged shots.
2.pvpDescription: Long firing lanes and narrow passages for accurate shots and ambushes.

3.title: Broken fortress
3.challengeDescription: Scattered cover forces you to keep changing your route.
3.pvpDescription: Scattered cover requires constant route and attack-direction changes.

sidebar.title: API COMMANDS
sidebar.for: Repeat a fixed number of times

instruction.title: Tank controls
instruction.intro: You control the tank with a program, not keyboard keys. Commands run from top to bottom. In arena mode, the prepared sequence repeats until the battle ends.
instruction.ifText: Runs nested commands only when the condition is true.
instruction.forText: Repeats a nested block a specified number of times.
instruction.whileText: Repeats a block while its condition remains true. Use it carefully.
instruction.grid: Grid: move() advances the tank exactly one cell.
instruction.brick: Brick: breaks with one hit and stops that projectile.
instruction.steel: Steel: blocks the tank and cannot be destroyed.
instruction.collision: Collision: two tanks cannot occupy the same cell.
instruction.victory: Victory: complete the mission objective or destroy the opponent.

move.description: Moves the tank one cell forward in the barrel's direction.
move.details: The tank cannot move through the map boundary, a wall, or another tank.

rotate.description: Rotates the tank 90° in the selected direction.
rotate.details: rotate('LEFT') turns left and rotate('RIGHT') turns right. rotate() also turns right.

scan.description: Checks whether an enemy is in the line of fire.
scan.details: Scanning works horizontally and vertically. Walls block visibility.

fire.description: Fires a projectile in the tank barrel's direction.
fire.details: A projectile damages tanks. The first brick block in its path is destroyed and stops the projectile.
